title: Timeline Column Not Showing Battery
excerpt:
I created a board and the timeline column is supposed to have a battery by default. 
However the timeline column on my board does not have a battery, it’s just grey. 
  
Anyone knows how to activate it?

posts.author: UniceTanAlchemy (Unice Tan)
posts.cookedHtml:
I created a board and the timeline column is supposed to have a battery by default.

However the timeline column on my board does not have a battery, it’s just grey.

image1060×120 13.8 KB

Anyone knows how to activate it?

posts.author: hussy113344 (razahussy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Here are some steps you can try to troubleshoot the problem:

Note Muts use story saver

  1. Refresh the Page: Sometimes, simply refreshing the page can resolve display issues. Try refreshing your browser to see if the battery icon appears.
  2. Check Column Settings: Make sure that the timeline column is set up correctly. Check if the battery option is enabled in the column settings. You may need to edit the column settings to enable the battery display.
  3. Clear Cache: Clearing your browser cache can sometimes help resolve display issues. Try clearing your browser cache and then reload the page to see if the battery icon appears.
  4. Update Software: Ensure that you are using the latest version of the software or app. Sometimes, updates include fixes for display issues.
  5. Contact Support: If none of the above steps work, it might be a technical issue specific to your account or the platform. Contact the support team for assistance. They can provide further troubleshooting steps or fix the issue on their end.

By following these steps, you should be able to troubleshoot and resolve the issue with the battery icon not appearing in your timeline column. Let me know if you need further assistance!

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @UniceTanAlchemy

The timeline shows the (in two colors) the period that has passed and the period yet to come. If the complete timeline is in the future you will just see one color. In this case… wait and see how it looks at April 24th

posts.author: UniceTanAlchemy (Unice Tan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @basdebruin thank you for the advice! i tried to set it to 17 - 19 April just to test out your theory and it works! it’s in red if the time period enters the timeline.

image783×84 9.97 KB

I was wondering how to get it to have a green battery leading up to the timeline eg. if my event is from 23 - 26 April, from now till the start of the green battery it will slowly fill the timeline space with green.

Thank you!

posts.author: kaylak (Kayla K)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @UniceTanAlchemy ,

The color of the bar is actually determined by the color of the group it is in. So if your group is red, the color will be red, if your group is blue, the color will be blue.

Have you checked out “deadline mode” - Maybe this is something that could help get the colors to match what you are looking for.
Deadline Mode – Support.
The monday documentation will be able to explain it better than me, so I’ve linked that above.

Hope this helps!

posts.author: martinop (Martin Willie)
posts.cookedHtml: Hassan! Thanks for sharing your valuable knowledge here.

posts.author: pearlholland (pearl holland)
posts.cookedHtml:
Here are a few steps you can take to troubleshoot this issue:

  1. Refresh the Page: Sometimes, a simple refresh of the page can resolve display issues. Try refreshing your browser and see if the battery icon appears.
  2. Check Column Settings: Verify the settings of the timeline column in your board. There might be an option to enable or customize the display of the battery icon.
  3. Update App Version: Ensure that you are using the latest version of the Monday.com app. Updates often include bug fixes and improvements that could resolve display issues.
  4. Contact Support: If the issue persists, reaching out to Monday.com’s support team would be the best course of action. They can provide personalized assistance and troubleshoot the problem further.

I hope these suggestions help you resolve the issue with the timeline column. Let us know if you need any further assistance!

Best regards, Invisible Text

posts.author: martinop (Martin Willie)
posts.cookedHtml: My issue fixed when I read this and apply method.

posts.author: Albertjonson (Albert Jonson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Unice Tan:

I created a board and the timeline column is supposed to have a battery by default.

However the timeline column on my board does not have a battery, it’s just grey.

Causes and Solutions:

  1. No Dates Assigned:
  • The “battery” in a timeline column typically reflects the duration between start and end dates. If dates are not assigned to tasks or items in the column, it will remain grey.
  • Solution: Assign start and end dates to the items in the timeline column. Once dates are added, the battery should appear automatically.
  1. Incorrect Configuration:
  • The timeline column might not be properly configured to display the battery.
  • Solution: Check the column settings. Ensure it’s set up to display the timeline properly, and that any necessary configurations (such as timeline range) are in place.
  1. Software Bug or Glitch:
  • Sometimes, issues like this occur due to a software bug or version-specific glitch.
  • Solution: Update the software to the latest version, as updates often include bug fixes. If you’re already on the latest version, try refreshing or restarting your board.
  1. Theme or Customization Conflict:
  • Custom themes or UI modifications might interfere with the visual display of the battery.
  • Solution: Revert to default settings or themes to ensure no conflicts are causing the issue.
  1. Compatibility Issues:
  • If you’re accessing the board on a mobile device or an outdated browser, it could affect how elements like the battery are displayed.
  • Solution: Access the board using a supported browser or device to verify if it works properly.

Regards Albert Jonson (PpSsPp)
